name: Pupukea-Paumalu Trail (Ted Makalena)
tagline: A mellow North Shore forest walk through ironwoods and native plants — a breather between surf sessions.
parking: Free parking at the trailhead on Pupukea Road across from the Boy Scout Camp
bestTime: Any time — it's shaded and low-key
talkStory: Named after Ted Makalena, a beloved Hawaiian pro golfer who died young in 1976, this trail system was developed by the North Shore community to preserve 1,100 acres of forest from development. The land contains remnants of ancient Hawaiian agricultural terraces and stone walls from pre-contact cultivation. The ironwood and Cook pine canopy creates a shaded, cathedral-like atmosphere that locals call the North Shore's best 'forest bathing' spot. Perfect for a mellow walk or trail run between surf sessions — don't expect summit views, this is about the trees.
